content.full_text:
Fern PolrrrcAt- Pnecrrcps CoMMrssroN
.128 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacrarnenro. CA 95gl.l-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) -122-0886
June 8, 2009
Willie L. Hailey Sr.
Councilmember, City of Barstow
1160 Barstow Road, #11
Barstow, Califomia 92311
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09-139
Dear Mr. Hailey:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r This letter is based on the facts
presented; the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a
finder of fact when it renders advice. (ln re oglesby (1975) I Fppc ops. 71.)
Additionally,'our advice is limited to obligations arising under the Act. We do
not address the applicability, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common
law conflict of interest or Government Code Section 1090. We strongly recommend that
you discuss the issue of common law conflict of interest with your city attorney or private
counsel.
QIIESTION
Do you have a conflict of interest under the Act in participating in a governmental
decision regarding an appeal of the City of Barstow's termination of your childhood
friend whom you helped initiate the appeal process?
CONCLUSION
Because the Act only concems conflicts of interest with respect to financial
interests, and you have no financial interest in this matter, you do nbt have a conflict of
interest under the Act.
' Government Code sectittns 81000 - glOl4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
I 8 109- 18997. of the California Code of Regulations.


File No.A-09-139
Page No. 2
FACTS2
You were elected to the Barstow City Council on November 4, 2008, and took
office on December 15, 2008. Before your election, you helped a childhood friend,
Victor Yslas, who was terminated from his position as a laborer for the City of Barstow
after receiving injuries while doing road repair for the city, with his initial appeal of the
termination.
Mr. Yslas is now represented by the San Bernardino Public Employee Association
and their attomey. An arbitration hearing was held on January 25 and29,2OO9. On
June 8, 2009, the city council will be asked to accept or reject the findings of the
arbitrator.
You did not receive any compensation for you assistance with Mr. Yslas' initial
appeal, and you have no financial interest in the outcome of the decision.
AIIALYSIS
PorrNrllt, CoNFLrcr oF INTEREST
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will "perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests
or the financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 81001(b).)
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which
the official has a financial interest.
The Commission has adopted an eight-step standard analysis for deciding whether
an official has a disqualifying conflict of interest. (Regulation 18700(b).) The gineral
rule, however, is that a conflict of interest exists whenever a public official makes a
governmental decision that has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one
or more of his or her financial interests.
srrps I & 2t Anr you A punlrc orprcrlt M.lKrrrrc, plnrrcparrNc rN
MlxtNG, oR INFLUENCING A GovERNMENTAL DccrstoN?
As a member of the Barstow City Council, you are a public official under the Act.
(Section 82048.) Consequently, you may not make, participate in making, or otherwise use
her official position tp influence any decisions that will have a reasonably foreseeable
material financial effect on any of your economic interests. You will be Called upon to
consider approving an arbitration decision. Therefore, you will be making, participating in
making, or otherwise using your official position to influence a governmental decision.
I Additional information was provided in our telephone callof June 4,2oog.


File No.A-09-139
Page No. 3
SrBp3: Do You HevB A PoTENTIALLY Drsgu,lr,mYING ECoNoMIC lxrenosr?
A public official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of
section 87103 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a
member of his or her immediate family, or on any one of five enumerated economic
interests, including:
An economic interest in a business entity in which he or she has a direct or
indirect investment of $2,000 or more (section 87r03(a); Regulation
18703.1(a)); or in which he or she is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management. (Section g7103(d);
Regulation I 8703. I (b).)
An economic interest in real property in which he or she has a direct or
indirect interest of $2,000 or more. (Section 87103(b); Regulation
18703.2.)
a
o
a
An economic interest in any source of income, including promised
income, aggregating $500 or more within 12 months prioi to the decision.
(Section 87103(c); Regulation 18703.3.)
An economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if the gifts
aggregate to $420 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section
87 103(e); Regulation 187 03.4.)
o An economic interest in his or her personal finances, including those of his
or her immediate family -- this is the "personal financial effecis" rule.
(Section 87 103; Regularion 1 8703.5.)
You have indicated that you have not received any compensation for your work
on this matter, either actual or promised, and you have no financial interest in the
outcome of the matter nor will you benefit financially from participating in the decision.
Because the Act only regulates conflicts of interest with respect to financial
interests, and you have not indentified any financial interest in the gouernmental decision,
you do not have a conflict of interest under the Act, and our analysis ends at this point.
We strongly recommend, however, that you discuss this issue with your city attorney or
private counsel with respect to a potential conflict of interest under common law conflict
of interest principles.


File No.A-09-139
Page No.4
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Counsel
By:
Lenkeit
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
wJLjgl
